summary: Re-derive the F44 re-open lockdown wedge mechanism under relaxed GSP locking + finalize the A10-v2 fix now that C6 corrects the COND_ACQUIRE primitive

findings: relaxed locking frees the API lock before the poll; worker holds only the GPU-group lock (kernel_gsp.c:4785, 4861-5155)
foreground holds ldata_lock (nv.c:2119); rm_cleanup acquire succeeds (osapi.c:1906), not the deadlock edge
worker stays in poll: osIsGpuBusDead false (os.c:1884), chip answers MMIO, no C2 marker on the A6-only build
wedge is rmmod blocking on ldata_lock (nv-pci.c:2426) behind the parked foreground in flush_work; AER nv-pci.c:2968 alt
only C2 frees the worker via cond-true (gh100.c:540), not C1 (API lock free); A10-v2 entry-arm fails, poll-entry after 200ms read, arm at osapi.c:1761
fileRefs: kernel_gsp.c:4785, gh100.c:540, osapi.c:1906, os.c:1884, nv.c:2119, nv-pci.c:2426
openQuestions: analyze kdump for parked tasks; verify gpuTimeout unbounded; prove C2 unwind

findings: No A10 row in the manifest so A10 C1 and C2 are not deployed, the A6 timeout branch lacks os_pci_set_disconnected, C6 is deployed via base row 1f196b61 but the a5 source still shows it inverted, A11 is the first live C6 consumer

Relaxed locking at kernel_gsp.c 4783 releases the API lock before kgspBootstrap 4798 so across the gh100 1050 poll the worker holds ldata lock and the group lock but not the API write lock, the deadlock RCA and the a10 and A7 in-tree comments are false

Q2 rm_cleanup COND osapi.c 1913 defers on contention without sinking, Q4 cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic os.c 1921 itself calls os_pci_set_disconnected 1942 so on WPR2 fast fail C1 succeeds and C5 sinks even with C2 gated, gating only C2 is insufficient must also cover C5

Q3 C2 containment is lock independent via osIsGpuBusDead os.c 1884 with no lock, Q1 the wedge is the foreground holding ldata lock nv.c 1867 across the blocking C5 NONE osapi.c 995 and flush work while the worker is stuck not API lock held across poll

Minimal fix gates C2 and C5 to the lockdown substrate armed after the WPR2 check near kernel_gsp.c 4771, the entry arm at 941 proof is dead, a false negative does not re-wedge only if A10 C1 is deployed but apnex-27 has neither so it is unprotected
openQuestions: A10 is not in the manifest so apnex-27 lacks C2 and the C1 floor, add an A10 row carrying C1 plus gated C2 plus gated C5 or fold into A6 A7
Use the 2026-06-02 vmcore to confirm whether the wedge contends ldata lock or the group lock
Confirm whether the lost prop is already set on the WPR2 fast fail chip when C5 runs which decides if the gate must also cover C5

findings: C1 under C6 alone breaks F44 into a soft wait (worker holds group lock not API lock); C2 only shortens; deep-RM flag unnecessary; gate C2/C5 by worker-not-completed.

Relaxed locking frees the API lock across the poll so the deadlock RCA is false the real wedge is the foreground in flush_work holding ldata_lock while rmmod or AER blocks C1 rm_cleanup COND_ACQUIRE under corrected C6 cuts it via the lock-free dead-bus marker so the flag and grace are unnecessary

Keep C2 gated because the 200ms timeout also fires on the WPR2-up fast-fail where ungated C2 sinks the bus verify PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST set on a transient fail else C1 itself sinks keep A7 C2 unconditional preserve flush_work add the A10 manifest row C6 base first
openQuestions: verify PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST set on a transient fast-fail else C1 needs the gate ship C1-only first validate via passive self-heal and drgn

critical: FALSE-PREMISE #3 (the one you warned me to expect) — the DESIGN'S OWN SAFETY FLOOR ('C1 alone cuts the wedge; flag and grace unnecessary') RESTS ON AN UNVERIFIED 'self-heal' CLAIM THAT THE DEPLOYED LOGS FALSIFY. The DESIGN keeps C5 rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state OUTSIDE any gate at nv.c:1919 and runs it on EVERY A6 200ms timeout. I traced the fast-fail: _kgspBootGspRm bails WPR2-up at kernel_gsp.c:4768 -> RmInitAdapter fails -> osinit.c:2371 `shutdown:` -> RmShutdownAdapter -> `failed:` -> returns NV_FALSE. That clean init-failure path NEVER sets PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST. Forensics confirm the fast-fail crosses the 200ms budget (r2: open scheduled 5322.943, timed out 5323.149 = 205.6ms, RmInitAdapter-failed 5323.151 = +207.9ms), so the worker has RETURNED by the time the foreground runs C5, the API lock is FREE, and (with C1+C6) the COND_ACQUIRE SUCCEEDS -> cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic (os.c:1921) sees PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST==FALSE at os.c:1935 -> does NOT skip -> calls os_pci_set_disconnected(nv->handle) at os.c:1942, which writes pci_channel_io_perm_failure (os-pci.c:183) — a SINK STATE WITH NO TRANSITIONS OUT. So C5 PERMANENTLY DEAD-BUSES THE CHIP ON EVERY FAST-FAIL, with or without C2. The DESIGN's openQuestion ('verify PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST set on a transient fast-fail else C1 itself sinks') is the actual crux and the answer is: it is NOT set, so C1/C5 DOES sink. Gating only C2 (nv.c:1914) does NOT restore fast-fail recoverability — C5 at nv.c:1919 sinks it anyway. This is a REGRESSION the DESIGN ships, not a fix.

THE 'DEPLOYED SELF-HEAL' EVIDENCE THE DESIGN INHERITS IS FABRICATED/MISREAD. I checked the deployed manifest (/root/nvidia-driver-injector/patches/manifest: C6,C1-C5,E1,A1-A9,A11 — NO A10) and the rung4 deployed timeline directly. The `open completed within budget rc=0` lines occur ONLY on the HEALTHY chip BEFORE the first sink (rung4: 34912.5 rc=0 opens, then clean shutdown clears WPR2, then 34916.18 re-open TIMES OUT -> sink -> RmInitAdapter failed -> 'trigger gated (sink-set: GPU already declared lost (C5 sink)); emitting PERMANENT_FAIL'). There is NO rc=0 open AFTER a sink anywhere in r2/r3/r4 (65 PERMANENT_FAIL events; recovery in every case was an EXTERNAL `fix-bar1` rebind that recreates the pci_dev, not in-driver self-heal). No code writes pci_channel_io_normal anywhere in kernel-open — the sink is genuinely permanent until module/pci_dev reload. So 'deployed driver self-heals the fast-fail' (forensics doc:37, design v1delta, the DESIGN's finding-2) is FALSE. The DESIGN's claim that gating C2 'preserves self-heal' has nothing to preserve — and worse, the fast-fail self-heal it wants NEVER EXISTED in-driver, meaning the fast-fail 'regression' is real but its baseline is mischaracterized.

RE-WEDGE under 'C1 alone' on the LOCKDOWN substrate + AER/rmmod second-contender. The DESIGN drops the lock-free C2 marker to a gate of undefined condition ('keep C2 gated' while simultaneously 'the flag is unnecessary' — internally contradictory) and leans on 'C1 + flush_work.' But the containment that makes flush_work join FAST on the lockdown substrate is EXACTLY the lock-free os_pci_set_disconnected marker (C2 at nv.c:1914, set BEFORE flush_work) short-circuiting the worker's poll reads via osIsGpuBusDead (os.c:1884) so _kgspLockdownReleasedOrFmcError (gh100.c:540, mailbox0=0xFFFFFFFF) returns NV_TRUE and the worker exits in one iteration. If C2's marker is NOT reliably set on the lockdown substrate, C1 alone does NOT free the worker: C5's cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic also calls os_pci_set_disconnected, BUT under relaxed locking the worker holds the GPU GROUP lock across the poll (kernel_gsp.c:4746 reacquire only AFTER), and gpuSetDisconnectedProperties queues a work item with .bLockGpuGroupDevice=NV_TRUE (gpu.c:5286-5290) that blocks behind that group lock — while os_pci_set_disconnected at os.c:1942 runs AFTER gpuSetDisconnectedProperties at os.c:1938 in the same function. So whether C5 marks the bus before the worker's next poll read is timing-dependent, not guaranteed-immediate like the dedicated C2 line. With C2 effectively removed, the foreground blocks in flush_work holding ldata_lock for up to the full gpuTimeout (~4s gfx / ~30s compute), and a concurrent nv_pci_remove/rmmod (nv-pci.c:2456 down(ldata_lock) across nv_shutdown_adapter:2570) OR the AER error_detected path blocks behind ldata_lock -> the exact F44 host-wedge. 'C1 alone suffices' does NOT survive this interleaving.
concerns: The DESIGN correctly identifies the relaxed-locking falsification (API lock released at kernel_gsp.c:4785, reacquired 4818-4820 via _kgspBootReacquireLocks; _kgspShouldRelaxGspInitLocking returns NV_TRUE by default 0x2 with SPDM off) and correctly concludes the original 'worker holds API lock across the poll' deadlock RCA is false — I verified all of this against source. It also correctly notes the wedge is the foreground in flush_work holding ldata_lock while a second contender blocks. Those re-derivations are RIGHT. The error is the leap to 'therefore C1 alone suffices' without auditing C5's own sink and the lockdown-substrate containment dependency on C2.

Lock-state at the 200ms read is now precisely characterizable and the DESIGN/reviewers got the shape right: during the gh100.c:1050 poll the worker holds ldata_lock-equivalent (it is the worker, foreground holds nvl->ldata_lock) + the GPU subdevice GROUP lock, but NOT the RM API write-lock. The foreground's API-lock contention the original wedge RCA saw can only be the PRE-bootstrap / POST-reacquire RmInitAdapter window (osapi.c:1761 NONE acquire across RmInitAdapter, minus the relaxed release). C1+C6 makes the foreground's C5 rm_cleanup non-blocking against THAT window (defers cleanly) — this part of C1 is genuinely load-bearing and correct, and also fixes the AER error_detected callsite (nv-pci.c:2998) and A2 QWD callers which share rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state.

C6 is NOT in the a10 git ancestry (git merge-base --is-ancestor: NO) — the branches are independent single-feature branches off tag 595.71.05, composed only at the manifest/patch-stack layer. So at SOURCE level the a10 branch's C1 COND_ACQUIRE still calls the INVERTED os_cond_acquire_rwlock_write (returns NV_ERR_TIMEOUT_RETRY on acquire / NV_OK on contention). C1 only functions when C6 is composed ABOVE it in the apply order (manifest lists C6 first). Any A10 build/validation MUST verify C6 is in the composed stack or C1 corrupts the rwsem exactly as task #296 found. The DESIGN assumes C6-composed but should state this as a hard build-gate.

The A7 shutdown-path decision ('keep C2 unconditional') is defensible — the shutdown worker runs rm_shutdown_adapter (osapi.c:1852), NOT kgspBootstrap, so it has no WPR2-fast-fail twin and the in-lockdown-poll flag would be structurally FALSE there. BUT the A7 in-tree comment (nv.c:2316-2321) repeats the SAME falsified 'shutdown worker stalls in a GSP poll holding the RM API lock' premise; the shutdown ~600ms is a GSP unload RPC, a different lock regime that was NOT audited here. A7 staying unconditional is correct but its rationale text is built on the same wrong lock model and should be re-derived, not inherited.

The in-tree A10-v1 comment at nv.c:1925-1956 (and its a6-branch twin) ASSERTS the false mechanism verbatim: 'rm_cleanup takes a BLOCKING acquire (API_LOCK_FLAGS_NONE)' and 'the worker holds that API lock for the whole GSP-lockdown poll.' On a10 HEAD d2a4e514 osapi.c:1906 is already COND_ACQUIRE, so the comment is stale on TWO counts (blocking->COND, and worker-holds-lock->worker-released-it-under-relaxed-locking). Any v2 must rewrite this comment or it perpetuates the false premise into the next cycle — which is exactly how the last two proofs propagated.
suggestedChanges: DO NOT SHIP 'C1 alone.' The minimal correct fix must ALSO suppress C5's permanent sink on the fast-fail substrate, because C5/cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic itself calls os_pci_set_disconnected (os.c:1942) and PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST is NOT set on a transient WPR2-up fast-fail (verified: clean RmShutdownAdapter teardown, osinit.c:2371-2389). Either (a) gate BOTH C2 (nv.c:1914) AND C5 (nv.c:1919) on the same lockdown-substrate signal, or (b) skip rm_cleanup entirely on the A6 fast-fail timeout and only run the lock-free marker on the genuine lockdown substrate. The reviewers' 'final' verdict (gate BOTH C2 and C5 + grace re-wait, confidence MEDIUM) is closer to correct than the DESIGN's 'C1 alone' (which the evidence falsifies).

RE-BASELINE the regression claim against reality before designing around it: the deployed build does NOT self-heal the fast-fail in-driver — it sinks the bus on the FIRST fast-fail (C5 via cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic) and recovers only via external fix-bar1 rebind (65 PERMANENT_FAIL events in r2-r4). So 'A10-v1's unconditional C2 introduced a fast-fail regression vs a self-healing baseline' is the wrong framing — C5 already sank the fast-fail in the deployed build. The genuine improvement target is: make the fast-fail RECOVERABLE in-driver for the first time (suppress BOTH C2 and C5 sink on the transient fast-fail), which is strictly more than 'C1 alone' delivers.

RESOLVE the substrate-discriminator at a lock-correct, source-boundary-aligned arm point. The WPR2 check at kernel_gsp.c:4763-4768 is the ONLY hard boundary that separates fast-fail (returns at 4768) from lockdown (passes 4768, enters kgspBootstrap). Arm the discriminator immediately AFTER the WPR2-pass at ~4771 (before kgspPopulateWprMeta), clear it at kgspInitRm completion — NOT at gh100.c:941 (reviewers proved 941 is reached AFTER 200ms on the lockdown substrate, so an entry-arm reads FALSE at the foreground read and SUPPRESSES containment on the substrate that needs it — a re-wedge). Even with a correct arm point, ADD the bounded grace re-wait the DESIGN omitted: the lockdown poll-entry lands just past 200ms, so a single ~50-150ms re-wait + re-read before deciding to skip the sink catches the TRUE transition while the fast-fail worker (returned by ~207ms) stays FALSE. The DESIGN's claim that grace is 'unnecessary' depends on the falsified entry-before-200ms proof.

VERIFY C6 IS COMPOSED IN THE BUILD as a hard gate (manifest lists C6-cond-acquire-rwlock-fix first; git ancestry does NOT carry it into a10). Without C6, C1's COND_ACQUIRE hits the inverted primitive and corrupts the global RM API rwsem (task #296). Add a build-time assertion or a composed-source check before any A10 validation.

REWRITE the stale/false in-tree comments in the SAME commit: nv.c:1925-1956 (A6) and nv.c:2316-2321 (A7) both assert 'rm_cleanup takes a BLOCKING acquire' and 'worker holds the RM API lock across the GSP poll.' Replace with the verified model: under relaxed GSP init locking the worker RELEASES the API lock at kernel_gsp.c:4785 before the poll and holds only the GPU GROUP lock; C1+C6 makes the foreground's rm_cleanup non-blocking; the lock-free os_pci_set_disconnected marker is what frees the worker from the poll. Also correct the forensics doc (wedge-2026-06-02 line 33-37) and the 'deployed self-heal' claim (line 37) — both carry the falsified premises forward.

VALIDATION GATE before flipping to production: (1) deterministic fast-fail substrate n>=3 — assert error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal (chip recoverable in-driver WITHOUT fix-bar1 rebind) AND a subsequent open returns rc=0; this is the BLOCKING gate and it directly tests the C5-sink regression I flagged. (2) lockdown substrate at least one fire (best-effort until fake-5090 task #290) — worker self-terminates one poll iteration, flush_work joins, -EIO, host alive, ldata_lock released promptly under a concurrent rmmod. (3) confirm C6 in the composed stack.

critical: DESIGN's finding #1 is INTERNALLY CONTRADICTED and partly UNVERIFIED. It says 'ship C1 alone' in the summary but its own finding #2 open-question concedes C1-alone may itself sink the bus on fast-fail, and the design body then says 'keep C2 gated' + 'add the A10 manifest row' (i.e. ship C1+C2-gated, NOT C1 alone). The summary 'A10-v2 ship C1 alone' does not match the body. More importantly: C1 ALONE IS NOT A CONTAINMENT for F44. Verified: with relaxed locking the worker holds NO API lock across the gh100.c:1050 poll (kernel_gsp.c:4785 rmapiLockRelease before kgspBootstrap_HAL at 4798, reacquire at 4818-4820), so the foreground's rm_cleanup COND_ACQUIRE (C1) SUCCEEDS immediately and runs cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic (os.c:1921). C1 by itself does NOT make the worker leave the multi-second poll. What makes the worker self-terminate is the LOCK-FREE dead-bus marker (C2 os_pci_set_disconnected, os.c:1942 / nv.c:1914) feeding osIsGpuBusDead (os.c:1892) -> osDevReadReg032 returns 0xFFFFFFFF (os.c:2054, nv-gpu-lost.h:47) -> _kgspLockdownReleasedOrFmcError returns NV_TRUE via the mailbox0!=0 clause (gh100.c:524/540-541). Without C2 the lockdown poll runs to full gpuTimeout while the foreground blocks in flush_work (nv.c:1957) holding ldata_lock (taken at nv.c:2113, released nv.c:2133). So the containment is C2 (now reachable because cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic takes NO API lock and NO GPU group lock - verified), NOT C1. C1 only de-risks the SECONDARY blocking-acquire callers (AER nv-pci.c:2968, A2 QWD). Shipping 'C1 alone' would leave F44 UNCONTAINED.

DESIGN's open question 'verify PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST set on a transient fast-fail else C1 itself sinks' resolves AGAINST the design - and this is a SHIP-BLOCKING regression, not an open question. Verified end-to-end: on a transient WPR2-up fast-fail the chip is healthy and NOTHING sets PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST (it is set ONLY by gpuSetDisconnectedProperties at gpu.c:5272 via cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic, or osHandleGpuLost retry-exhausted at osinit.c:442 - neither fires on a clean NV_ERR_INVALID_STATE WPR2 return from kernel_gsp.c:4768). The fast-fail worker returns ~205-210ms (just past the 200ms A6 budget), so by the time the foreground timeout branch reaches C5 rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state (nv.c:1919) the worker has returned and the API lock is FREE -> the COND_ACQUIRE (or even the deployed blocking NONE) SUCCEEDS -> cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic runs the os.c:1935 gate, finds PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST CLEAR, and calls gpuSetDisconnectedProperties (os.c:1938) + os_pci_set_disconnected (os.c:1942), PERMANENTLY sinking a recoverable chip. Therefore gating ONLY the explicit C2 line at nv.c:1914 does NOT restore fast-fail self-heal: C5's INTERNAL os_pci_set_disconnected is a SECOND, independent sink on the fast-fail path. The two independent design reviewers (a10-v2 JSON lines 172, 193) and the 'final' synthesis (lines 235-236) all caught this and concluded C5 must ALSO be gated. The DESIGN under review treats it as an unresolved openQuestion and ships anyway - that is the A10-v1 regression re-introduced.

DESIGN's claim that 'the real wedge is the foreground in flush_work holding ldata_lock while rmmod or AER blocks C1 rm_cleanup' is NOT established and is mechanically shaky. Verified: (a) cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic / gpuSetDisconnectedProperties take NO GPU group lock (grep of os.c) and rm_cleanup's acquire is API-lock-only, so the foreground rm_cleanup does NOT contend the GPU subdevice group lock the worker DOES hold across the poll (kgspInitRm acquires it at kernel_gsp.c:4860, relaxed path keeps it across the poll). (b) The AER nv_pci_error_detected handler (nv-pci.c:2901) does NOT take ldata_lock before calling rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state (nv-pci.c:2968); it calls the SAME function the foreground does, so under relaxed locking BOTH find the API lock free and neither blocks on the worker. (c) The forensic 'foreground deadlocked in rm_cleanup blocking acquire AT 200ms' (wedge doc step 3) is the FALSIFIED premise - under relaxed locking the API lock is free at the poll, so that specific deadlock CANNOT be the mechanism as stated. The actual host-loss mechanism is therefore most plausibly: WITHOUT C2 the worker never leaves the poll, the foreground blocks in flush_work (nv.c:1957) up to gpuTimeout HOLDING ldata_lock, and EVERY subsequent ldata_lock acquirer (close nv.c:2224, a second open, nv_pci_remove_helper nv-pci.c:2426/2487, sysfs) piles up behind it -> with hung-task detection unset this presents as a silent total wedge (soft, not a classic rwsem deadlock). The DESIGN asserts this mechanism as settled fact while simultaneously claiming C1 cuts it - but C1 does NOT shorten the flush_work/gpuTimeout wait (the worker is in a chip-answering poll, not a dead-bus one) UNLESS C2 has marked the bus. So the design's own stated wedge requires C2 to break, contradicting 'ship C1 alone'.
concerns: DESIGN's CORRECT and valuable core insight (which the verdict should preserve): the relaxed-locking finding genuinely falsifies the original F44 deadlock RCA and the A10-v2 'entry-arm race-proof', and a large part of the elaborate worker-in-lockdown-poll FLAG + 150ms grace-rewait machinery WAS built to work around (i) the now-fixed inverted C6 primitive and (ii) a false 'worker holds API lock across poll' lock model. With C6 corrected (verified on c6-cond-acquire-rwlock-fix branch: 'if (!down_read_trylock(...))' - the deployed tree's a5 branch still shows the inverted form at os-interface.c:337/349 but a5 simply doesn't carry C6; the composed apnex.27 build does) the COND_ACQUIRE primitive truthfully returns TIMEOUT_RETRY on contention. So the design is RIGHT that the smallest correct fix is much simpler than the full flag+grace A10-v2. It is wrong only about WHICH minimal pieces are load-bearing: the floor is C2-reachability (already reachable since cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic needs neither the API lock nor the GPU group lock), gated to avoid the fast-fail sink, applied to BOTH the explicit C2 marker AND C5's internal sink.

NAMING/STATE collisions that could mislead implementation: (1) the task's 'C1/C2/C5/C6' (F44-investigation names) collide with the patch MANIFEST's base-patch 'C1-kbuild-version-mk / C2-aer-internal-unmask / C5-crash-safety / C6-cond-acquire-rwlock-fix' - these are DIFFERENT things. The design's manifest-row advice ('add the A10 manifest row') must not be confused with the existing C-rows. (2) Deployed apnex.27 (manifest verified) contains C6-cond-acquire-rwlock-fix + A11-f45-deadlock-breaker but contains NO A10 row and NO A10 patch file - so F44 currently has ZERO in-driver containment deployed; A10-v1 was explicitly ABORTED (HEAD commit msg 'apnex.26 skipped = aborted A10-v1'). (3) The a10 branch line numbers (osapi.c:1913 C1, nv.c:1914 C2) differ from the deployed-tree osapi.c:1906 because the a10 branch adds a comment block; both refer to the same logical site.

A11 (F45 deferred-open COND_ACQUIRE on rm_get_adapter_status, gated by is_external_gpu, verified on a11-f45-deadlock-breaker:osapi.c ~2756) IS deployed and is a clean precedent for the same COND-or-bail pattern the F44 fix needs. The F45 design's 'composition' agent (f45 JSON line 110) claims relaxed locking REACQUIRES the API lock BEFORE the heartbeat-fail propagates and so is 'irrelevant to F45' - that conflicts with the f45 'cycle' agent (line 12) and the A10-v2 finding; the contradiction is unresolved in the F45 doc but does not change the F45 fix correctness (the F45 deadlocking acquire is rm_get_adapter_status, a path that runs AFTER init returns, where the lock is genuinely held by a concurrent actor). Worth noting only so the F44 author does not inherit the 'reacquire-before-propagate' framing - for the F44 OPEN-timeout window the API lock is provably FREE across the gh100.c:1050 poll (kernel_gsp.c:4785/4818).

Validation reality (from the forensics + reviewer concern): the lockdown substrate is STOCHASTIC (fires only when cycle-1 clears WPR2) and live re-runs are blocked on this very fix, so the lockdown-arm cannot be validated on-demand on hardware today; fake-5090 F44 model (task #290, deferred) is the realistic gate. The DETERMINISTIC, ship-blocking validation gate is the WPR2-fast-fail self-heal (n>=3): with the chosen fix, assert error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal AND the next open returns rc=0 AND drgn/sysfs shows PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST NOT set. The design's 'ship C1-only first, validate via passive self-heal and drgn' instinct is right; it just must include C5-sink suppression in the thing being shipped, or the self-heal test will FAIL.
suggestedChanges: Reframe the fix as 'C2-reachable + sink-suppressed-on-fast-fail', not 'C1 alone'. Minimal correct F44 fix = (1) keep C1 (rm_cleanup COND_ACQUIRE) - it is load-bearing for the AER/QWD secondary callers and is now functional post-C6, but it is NOT the F44 containment; (2) the actual containment is the LOCK-FREE C2 os_pci_set_disconnected at nv.c:1914 (the worker self-terminates the poll via osIsGpuBusDead/osDevReadReg032 0xFFFFFFFF -> gh100.c:541) - this stays; (3) the regression fix must suppress the dead-bus sink on the WPR2-fast-fail substrate for BOTH the explicit C2 line (nv.c:1914) AND C5's internal os_pci_set_disconnected inside cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic (os.c:1942) reached via rm_cleanup at nv.c:1919.

Pick the simplest substrate-discriminator that gates the SINK (not the poll). The cheapest source-aligned signal is the worker's COMPLETION state, which is already observable lock-free in the timeout branch: the fast-fail worker has RETURNED by ~207ms (its complete() fired ~2ms after the 200ms budget) whereas the lockdown worker is still in-flight. Concretely: on the A6 timeout, do one short bounded re-wait (the reviewers' grace, sized to the ~5-8ms overshoot, e.g. 30-50ms) on the SAME completion; if it now completes -> fast-fail, SKIP both C2 and C5-sink (self-heal preserved, chip stays pci_channel_io_normal); if it is still not complete -> genuine lockdown, apply C2 (and let C5 run). This is simpler and more robust than the nv_state_t worker-in-lockdown-poll flag + deep-RM gh100.c edits (which need 8+ macro-return clear sites and an os-abstraction helper to even compile - verified gh100.c does not include unix nv.h), and it side-steps the entire entry-arm-vs-poll-entry timing race that the falsified lock model created.

If a deep-RM substrate flag is still preferred over the completion-state grace, arm it AFTER the WPR2 check passes (kernel_gsp.c just after line 4768/4771 - the hard fast-fail/lockdown source boundary) so it is TRUE by construction ONLY on the lockdown substrate, and reach it from deep RM via an os-abstraction helper declared in src/nvidia/inc/kernel/os/os.h (already included by gh100.c) implemented in unix os.c (where NV_GET_NV_STATE is in scope, precedent osIsGpuBusDead os.c:1884) - do NOT insert NV_GET_NV_STATE(pGpu) directly in gh100.c (will not compile). But prefer the completion-state grace above; it needs zero deep-RM edits.

Gate the SINK suppression itself on is_external_gpu (mirroring A11) so dGPU behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged, and keep A7 SHUTDOWN-path C2 (nv.c:2322) UNCONDITIONAL (the shutdown worker runs rm_shutdown_adapter, never kgspBootstrap, so it has no fast-fail twin - a genuine A7 timeout is a real teardown hang). Also fix the two STALE in-tree comments in the same commit: nv.c:1941-1956 and nv.c:2316-2321 both assert 'worker holds the RM API write-lock across the poll' / 'C5 takes a blocking acquire and re-couples for gpuTimeout' - both are FALSE under relaxed locking + post-C6 COND_ACQUIRE; rewrite them to state the worker holds the GPU subdevice group lock (not the API lock) across the poll and that the containment is the lock-free C2 marker.

Before shipping, run the DETERMINISTIC gate: WPR2-fast-fail n>=3 asserting (a) error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal, (b) next open rc=0 self-heal, (c) PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST NOT set (drgn). Treat the lockdown-arm as best-effort-until-fake-5090 (task #290). Do NOT claim F44 'fixed' on the fast-fail self-heal alone - that substrate never exercises the containment; it only proves the de-regression. Add manifest row A10 (currently absent - verified manifest ends at A11) and an A10 patch-intent doc, and note apnex.27 already carries C6+A11 so the bump that adds A10 must re-verify the /lib/firmware/nvidia/595.71.05 symlink (task #294).

critical: FAST-FAIL REGRESSION IS REAL AND C1-ALONE DOES NOT FIX IT (the DESIGN's own openQuestion resolves AGAINST the DESIGN). Source-confirmed: the WPR2-fast-fail does NOT set PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST -- RmInitAdapter's boot-fail path uses `goto shutdown`/RmShutdownAdapter (osinit.c:2009-2238, clean teardown), and osHandleGpuLost only sets GPU_IS_LOST after retry-exhausted dead-bus MMIO (osinit.c:353/424), which the fast-fail (chip answers MMIO, just won't boot from WPR2-up) never reaches. Therefore cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic's idempotency short-circuit (os.c:1935 `if getProperty(PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST) return`) does NOT fire on a fast-fail, so it runs gpuSetDisconnectedProperties + os_pci_set_disconnected (os.c:1938-1942), and os_pci_set_disconnected writes the PERMANENT pci_channel_io_perm_failure sink (os-pci.c:183, 'once set, no transitions out'). C5 rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state is called UNCONDITIONALLY in the A6 timeout branch (deployed A6 patch line 127; nv.c:1919 on a10). The A6 default timeout is 200ms (nv.c:1819) and the fast-fail lands at ~205-210ms (forensic delta), so the fast-fail DOES enter the timeout branch. With C1 (COND_ACQUIRE), the fast-fail worker has already returned by ~207ms so the API lock is FREE, the COND succeeds, and C5 sinks the bus on the TRANSIENT fast-fail -- the exact A10-v1 permanent-dead-bus sin, reached via C5's internal os_pci_set_disconnected rather than via C2. 'Ship C1 alone + drop the gate' therefore re-introduces the fast-fail regression unless C5 itself is suppressed on the fast-fail substrate. The DESIGN flagged this as an openQuestion but its verdict ('ship C1 alone') does not honor the answer.

THE 'DEPLOYED SELF-HEALS ON FAST-FAIL' PREMISE IS UNVERIFIED AND LIKELY FALSE -- the regression PRE-EXISTS C1. The deployed apnex.27 A6 timeout branch (in the manifest) already calls rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state UNCONDITIONALLY with the stock blocking API_LOCK_FLAGS_NONE acquire (deployed A6 patch line 127, osapi.c:1906 = NONE not COND in the working tree). On a fast-fail the worker has returned, so even the BLOCKING acquire succeeds, C5 runs, and os_pci_set_disconnected sinks the bus -- TODAY, before C1. The deployed A6's own in-tree comment ('the worker holds that API lock for the whole GSP-lockdown poll') is the now-FALSIFIED pre-relaxed-locking lock model and must not be trusted as evidence of self-heal. So the claim that fast-fails currently self-heal (r2's 34 rc=0 re-opens) needs re-derivation: either those re-opens happened on a path that did NOT enter the A6 200ms timeout branch (e.g. completed within budget, jiffies_left>0), or the bus was being re-cleared elsewhere. Until that is reconciled, neither 'C1 regresses fast-fail' nor 'C1 is regression-free' can be asserted -- and the DESIGN asserts the latter without evidence. This must be settled with a passive fast-fail repro (n>=3) reading error_state + subsequent-open rc BEFORE any C1/A10 ship.
concerns: LOCKDOWN-PATH MECHANISM (the part the DESIGN gets right): C1-alone genuinely DOES break the lockdown wedge, confirming the DESIGN's core re-derivation. Source-confirmed lock model: under relaxed GSP init locking (_kgspShouldRelaxGspInitLocking returns NV_TRUE by default on Unix consumer 5090 -- SPDM off, regkey default branch, kernel_gsp.c:4783/4707-4718), _kgspBootGspRm RELEASES the API lock at kernel_gsp.c:4785 BEFORE kgspBootstrap_HAL (4798) and _kgspBootReacquireLocks reacquires it only AFTER the poll (4818-4820), having first released+reacquired the GPU group lock. So across the gh100.c:1050 lockdown poll the worker holds the GPU SUBDEVICE GROUP lock, NOT the API lock. The foreground's C5 rm_cleanup COND_ACQUIRE therefore finds the API lock FREE, succeeds, runs cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic -> os_pci_set_disconnected -> the worker's poll reads route osDevReadReg032 -> osIsGpuBusDead -> os_pci_is_disconnected = dead-bus 0xFFFFFFFF -> _kgspLockdownReleasedOrFmcError returns NV_TRUE (gh100.c:540 mailbox0!=0) -> worker self-terminates in one poll iteration. C5's OWN internal os_pci_set_disconnected IS the dead-bus marker -- so C2 is genuinely redundant for the lockdown path, and the worker-flag + grace-rewait are NOT needed. The DESIGN is correct that the complex A10-v2 machinery existed only to work around the (now-corrected by C6) broken cond primitive + the (now-falsified) API-lock-across-poll model. This is the smaller cut and is sound for the lockdown substrate.

RESIDUAL ldata_lock WEDGE under C1-alone is contained but conditional. The foreground holds nvl->ldata_lock (taken in nv_try_lock_foreground_open, nv.c:1867; released only at the post-open up() ~nv.c:2134) ACROSS flush_work(&w->work) (nv.c:1957). flush_work blocks until the worker leaves the poll. With C1+C5 sinking the bus, the lockdown worker self-terminates fast so flush_work joins quickly -> host alive. THIS IS THE LOAD-BEARING DEPENDENCY: any future change that GATES or SKIPS the C5 sink on the lockdown substrate (to fix the fast-fail regression) would leave the lockdown worker un-sunk, flush_work would then block for the full gpuTimeout (~4s/~30s) with ldata_lock HELD, and a concurrent rmmod/close/AER contending ldata_lock re-creates the wedge. So the minimal correct fix MUST keep C5 sinking on the lockdown substrate while NOT sinking on the fast-fail substrate -- i.e. it must distinguish the two substrates. C1-alone cannot distinguish them (both reach the same C5 call with a free lock), which is precisely why a substrate discriminator (a worker-state flag, or a WPR2-result signal, or skipping C5 when GPU_IS_LOST is unset AND the worker already returned cleanly) is NOT eliminable. The DESIGN's 'drop the gate entirely' is unsafe for exactly this reason.

DEEP-RM LINE COUNT vs kernel-open-only alternative: 'C1 alone' is a 1-line deep-RM change (osapi.c:1906 NONE->COND_ACQUIRE) and is the minimal deep-RM footprint -- strictly smaller than A10-v2's ~10-edit kgsp_gh100.c flag-arm + clear-on-8-early-exits + os-interface helper-pair + nv.h field. That is a genuine blast-radius win and aligns with sovereignty discipline. HOWEVER, because C1-alone does not solve the fast-fail regression, the regression-free minimal fix is NOT C1-alone; the smallest CORRECT cut is C1 (deep-RM, 1 line) PLUS a kernel-open-only fast-fail discriminator that conditions the A6-timeout C5 call (gate the rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state call itself, NOT C2 -- since the deployed branch has no C2). The discriminator can live entirely in kernel-open (nv.c A6 timeout branch): e.g. only call C5 when the worker is genuinely stuck (worker-not-returned at the timeout, which IS the lockdown substrate) and skip C5 when the worker already completed with a boot-fail rc (the fast-fail substrate). That keeps deep-RM at 1 line and puts the discriminator in the sovereign kernel-open layer -- lower blast radius than both A10-v2 and 'C1+ungated-C5'.

A7 SHUTDOWN DECISION is sound and should be kept unchanged. The shutdown worker runs rm_shutdown_adapter, never kgspBootstrap, so it is not on the lockdown-poll substrate and has no WPR2-fast-fail twin; its C5/sink behavior is orthogonal to the open-path regression. Keeping A7 untouched (no flag, no C1-coupling change beyond what C6 already corrected) is correct. Note only that A7's in-tree comment likely inherits the same falsified 'worker holds API lock across a GSP poll' lock model and should be reconciled for accuracy, but that is documentation, not a correctness bug.

COMPOSITION WITH C6/A11 IS CLEAN -- no contamination found. C6 (base, cond-primitive fix) is applied FIRST in the manifest (correct -- C1 depends on a correct cond primitive). A11 (F45) is on the deployed manifest and its patch file touches osapi.c rm_get_adapter_status (adds is_external_gpu-gated COND_ACQUIRE) and a small nv.c hunk -- it does NOT carry A10's unconditional C2 os_pci_set_disconnected, even though the a11 branch is stacked above a10 in git (the regen merge-base diff strips the inherited A10 delta). A10 is NOT in the manifest at all. So shipping C1 (and any A10-v2) will be a NEW manifest row; verify A11 stays stacked-consistent and the regen does not double-apply the A6 timeout hunk that both A6 and the a10-line share. A11's COND_ACQUIRE on rm_get_adapter_status (osapi.c:2752 site) is a DIFFERENT site from C1 (rm_cleanup, osapi.c:1906) -- no overlap, both depend on C6, composable. R0 flush_work UAF guard (nv.c:1957) is preserved in the deployed A6 and in a10; it is downstream/outside any gate and must stay -- confirmed intact.
suggestedChanges: DO NOT ship 'C1 alone' on the verdict as written. Re-scope the minimal fix to: C1 (osapi.c:1906 NONE->COND_ACQUIRE, deep-RM 1 line, depends on C6) PLUS a kernel-open-only substrate discriminator in the A6 timeout branch that conditions the C5 rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state call. The discriminator must sink on the lockdown substrate (worker still stuck in poll at timeout) and NOT sink on the WPR2-fast-fail substrate (worker already returned with a boot-fail rc by ~207ms). Concretely: at the A6 timeout, check whether the worker has completed (e.g. a 'worker_returned' / w->rc-populated signal, or a short bounded grace re-wait of ~20-50ms then re-test completion) -- if the worker has returned (fast-fail), SKIP the C5 sink and just flush_work+return -EIO so the chip stays pci_channel_io_normal and self-heals; if the worker has NOT returned (lockdown), CALL C5 so it sinks the bus and the worker self-terminates. This keeps deep-RM at 1 line and puts the discriminator in the sovereign kernel-open layer.

RESOLVE THE FAST-FAIL SELF-HEAL PREMISE WITH EVIDENCE BEFORE ANY SHIP. Run a passive WPR2-fast-fail repro (n>=3) on the DEPLOYED apnex.27 (which already has unconditional C5 with blocking acquire) and read pdev error_state + subsequent-open rc with passive tools only (no nvidia-smi/MMIO post-suspected-sink, per the no-RPC-on-broken-BAR1 lesson). Determine empirically: does the deployed driver sink the bus on a fast-fail that ENTERS the A6 200ms timeout branch, or do the observed rc=0 self-heals only occur when the open completes within budget (jiffies_left>0, never reaching C5)? This decides whether the fast-fail regression is pre-existing-and-live (then C1 is neutral on it but the discriminator is still required to make self-heal correct) or whether self-heal currently works via a path the C5 call doesn't reach. Do not assert 'C1 is regression-free' or 'deployed self-heals' until this is measured.

KEEP C5 SINKING ON THE LOCKDOWN SUBSTRATE as a hard invariant of any de-regression. The residual ldata_lock-held flush_work wedge is only contained because C5 sinks the bus and the lockdown worker self-terminates fast. Add an explicit assertion/test that on the lockdown substrate the worker self-terminates within one poll iteration (flush_work joins in <~50ms) and host stays alive; and that on the fast-fail substrate error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal AND the next open returns rc=0. Both gates must pass before flipping the version to production.

PRESERVE: R0 flush_work UAF guard (nv.c:1957) unchanged and outside any gate; A7 shutdown C2/sink unchanged (no flag); C6 applied first (base). Reconcile the stale lock-model comments (deployed A6 patch lines ~145-160 'worker holds that API lock for the whole GSP-lockdown poll'; nv.c:1941-1956 on a10; A7 comment) to the relaxed-locking reality (worker holds GPU GROUP lock not API lock across the poll; API lock released at kernel_gsp.c:4785, reacquired at 4818) in the same commit -- these comments are the source of the two prior false-premise proofs and will mislead the next reviewer if left.

MANIFEST/COMPOSITION: add the fix as a NEW addon row AFTER A11 (it is the A10-successor; A10 was never in the manifest). Regenerate via the merge-base diff so the inherited A6/A10 timeout hunk is not double-applied, and verify the regenerated patch's nv.c A6-timeout hunk composes cleanly with the existing A6 patch hunk (both touch the same branch). Confirm C1's osapi.c:1906 change does not collide with A11's osapi.c:2752 change (different functions -- no overlap expected) and that both compile against the real kernel tree (make modules), not just git apply --check, per project policy. Handle the apnex version bump + matching /lib/firmware/nvidia/<full-ver> symlink (task #294) at deploy.

final.summary: CORRECTED F44 MECHANISM (true cycle, relaxed locking): On a consumer 5090 (SPDM/CC off) relaxed GSP init locking is ON by default, so _kgspBootGspRm RELEASES the RM API lock at kernel_gsp.c:4785 BEFORE kgspBootstrap_HAL and reacquires it only AFTER, at 4818-4820. Across the gh100.c:1050 lockdown poll the open-worker therefore holds the GPU SUBDEVICE GROUP lock and the foreground holds nvl->ldata_lock (taken nv.c:1867/1940) — but NOT the API write-lock. The original 'foreground rm_cleanup deadlocks on the API lock the worker holds across the poll' RCA is FALSE: the API lock is free across the poll. The real wedge: on the WPR2-clear lockdown substrate the worker enters gpuTimeoutCondWait and will spin for the full gpuTimeout (~4s gfx/~30s compute, source-estimated not measured) because the WPR2-clear chip never releases lockdown; A6's 200ms timeout fires; the foreground then sits in flush_work(&w->work) (nv.c:1957) HOLDING ldata_lock until the worker leaves the poll; any second ldata_lock contender (rmmod via nv_pci_remove_helper down(&ldata_lock), a concurrent close/open, or the AER error_detected path) piles up behind ldata_lock and, with CONFIG_DETECT_HUNG_TASK unset, presents as a silent total host wedge. What makes the worker LEAVE the poll fast is the LOCK-FREE os_pci_set_disconnected dead-bus marker (its poll cond _kgspLockdownReleasedOrFmcError reads MMIO → osIsGpuBusDead → 0xFFFFFFFF → returns NV_TRUE via the mailbox0!=0 clause at gh100.c:540-541), NOT C1; C1 only makes the foreground/AER/QWD rm_cleanup non-blocking against the worker's PRE-bootstrap API-lock window. FINAL FIX (one line): ship C1 (rm_cleanup NONE→COND_ACQUIRE, now functional post-C6) PLUS a kernel-open-only completion-state discriminator in the A6 timeout branch that, on a worker still in-flight at timeout (lockdown), runs the lock-free dead-bus marker + C5 to self-terminate the poll, and on a worker already returned with a boot-fail rc (WPR2 fast-fail) SKIPS both the marker and C5 so the chip stays pci_channel_io_normal and self-heals — with a bounded ~50-150ms grace re-wait to absorb the ~5-8ms overshoot. NO deep-RM worker-in-lockdown-poll flag, NO gh100.c edits, NO 8-site clear burden. Keep A7 unconditional. confidence MEDIUM (the fast-fail self-heal gate is deterministically testable and BLOCKING; the lockdown-arm is best-effort until fake-5090 task #290).
findings: CUT EDGE = completion-state discriminator, NOT a deep-RM lockdown-poll flag and NOT 'C1 alone'. The worker-in-lockdown-poll flag + entry-arm did NOT survive: under relaxed locking the worker reaches kgspBootstrap entry (gh100.c:941) only AFTER ~205ms (it runs kgspPopulateWprMeta 4772, scrubber 4776, kgspPrepareForBootstrap 4780, rmapiLockRelease 4785, then FSP/target-mask/SPDM/initRPCs before the poll at 1050), so an entry-armed or poll-armed flag reads FALSE at the foreground 200ms read on the very substrate that needs containment → re-wedge. C6 fixing C1 dissolved most of the A10-v2 machinery (the flag+grace existed to work around the inverted primitive + the false 'worker holds API lock across poll' model), but C6-fixed-C1 ALONE is NOT sufficient (see safety-floor finding). The robust discriminator is the worker's COMPLETION state, observed lock-free in the timeout branch.

EXACT SITES (line numbers on a10 branch a10-f40b-lockfree-sink HEAD d2a4e514). (1) KEEP C1: src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/src/osapi.c:1906 rmapiLockAcquire NONE→COND_ACQUIRE in rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state — already present on a10; load-bearing for the AER error_detected callsite (nv-pci.c) + A2 QWD, and for the foreground's own rm_cleanup against the worker's pre-bootstrap API-lock window. Depends on C6 (manifest applies C6 first). (2) REPLACE the unconditional A10-v1 lines in the A6 OPEN timeout branch: the os_pci_set_disconnected at nv.c:1914 AND the rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state at nv.c:1919 must BOTH become conditional on 'worker still in-flight at timeout'. Concretely, in nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded's else/timeout branch (else opens nv.c:1895): on the wait_for_completion_timeout==0 path, do ONE bounded grace re-wait wait_for_completion_timeout(&w->done, msecs_to_jiffies(GRACE_MS)) sized ~50-150ms; if it now completes → FAST-FAIL: skip os_pci_set_disconnected AND skip rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state, just flush_work + rc=-EIO (chip stays pci_channel_io_normal, self-heals); if still not complete → LOCKDOWN: run os_pci_set_disconnected(nv->handle) THEN rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state(sp,nv,DETECTOR_AER_FATAL) so the worker's poll self-terminates, THEN flush_work joins fast. flush_work(&w->work) at nv.c:1957 and rc=-EIO stay UNCONDITIONAL and OUTSIDE the gate.

WHY both C2 and C5 must be gated together (DESIGN's fatal omission — all 3 reviews + the design's own internal reviewers caught it): RmInitAdapter's boot-fail path (osinit.c:2371 shutdown: → RmShutdownAdapter → 2379 failed: → returns NV_FALSE) does NOT set PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST (only gpuSetDisconnectedProperties at gpu.c:5272 or osHandleGpuLost retry-exhausted at osinit.c:~424 set it, neither fires on a clean WPR2-up NV_ERR_INVALID_STATE return from kernel_gsp.c:4768). So on a fast-fail the os.c:1935 idempotency gate is FALSE → cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic runs gpuSetDisconnectedProperties + os_pci_set_disconnected at os.c:1942 = the PERMANENT pci_channel_io_perm_failure sink (os-pci.c:183, no transition out). Therefore C5's OWN internal os_pci_set_disconnected sinks the bus on every fast-fail, with or without the explicit C2 line. Gating only C2 at nv.c:1914 (the DESIGN under review, and A10-v2's nv.c-only gate) does NOT restore fast-fail self-heal. The completion-discriminator gates the rm_cleanup CALL itself, so C5's internal sink is also suppressed on fast-fail. Verified: cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic takes neither the API lock nor any synchronous GPU group lock (gpuSetDisconnectedProperties only setProperty + ASYNC osQueueWorkItem), so on the lockdown substrate the foreground's gated C5 runs fine with the API lock free.

SAFETY FLOOR — C1 alone is NOT a containment for F44 (the DESIGN summary 'ship C1 alone' is wrong and is internally contradicted by its own body 'keep C2 gated'): with relaxed locking the API lock is free across the poll, so C1's COND_ACQUIRE succeeds immediately and runs cleanupGpuLostStateAtomic — but C1 does NOT make the worker leave the multi-second poll. Only the lock-free os_pci_set_disconnected marker does (poll cond reads MMIO → osIsGpuBusDead os.c:1892 → osDevReadReg032 returns 0xFFFFFFFF os.c:2054 → _kgspLockdownReleasedOrFmcError NV_TRUE gh100.c:540-541). Without the marker the lockdown worker runs to full gpuTimeout while the foreground blocks in flush_work HOLDING ldata_lock (taken nv.c:1867/1940, released only post-open ~nv.c:2134), and a concurrent rmmod (nv_pci_remove_helper down(&ldata_lock)) / close / AER blocks behind ldata_lock = the F44 wedge. The discriminator therefore MUST run the marker on the lockdown substrate (worker still in-flight) — this is a hard invariant.

THE FAST-FAIL REGRESSION GATE is the deterministic, BLOCKING ship gate, and its baseline must be re-stated: the deployed apnex.27 does NOT self-heal the fast-fail in-driver — it already sinks the bus on the FIRST fast-fail via C5's internal os_pci_set_disconnected (deployed A6 calls rm_cleanup unconditionally with blocking NONE at osapi.c:1906; on fast-fail the worker has returned by ~207ms so the acquire succeeds and C5 sinks). The rc=0 re-opens in r2/r3/r4 occurred on opens that completed WITHIN the 200ms budget (jiffies_left>0, never entering the timeout branch), recovered via external fix-bar1 rebind. So 'A10-v1 regressed a self-healing baseline' is mis-framed: the genuine improvement target is to make the fast-fail RECOVERABLE in-driver FOR THE FIRST TIME by skipping BOTH sinks on the transient fast-fail. Gate (n≥3, passive reads only per the no-RPC-on-broken-BAR1 lesson): error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal AND next open rc=0 AND PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST not set (drgn).

PRESERVE unchanged: C1 at osapi.c:1906 (COND_ACQUIRE, depends on C6); the R0 flush_work UAF guard at nv.c:1957 (mandatory, OUTSIDE any gate — flush_work blocks until the worker leaves nv_open_device_for_nvlfp so freed nvlfp/sp are not dereferenced); A8 nv_tb_egpu_f40b_fired(); A11 (rm_get_adapter_status COND_ACQUIRE at osapi.c:2740, is_external_gpu-gated, different function from C1, both depend on C6 — no overlap); the defensive WRITE_ONCE/reset is unnecessary in the completion-state variant (no nv_state_t flag). A7 SHUTDOWN-path os_pci_set_disconnected at nv.c:2322 stays UNCONDITIONAL: the shutdown worker runs rm_shutdown_adapter, never kgspBootstrap, so it has no WPR2-fast-fail twin and a genuine A7 timeout is a real teardown hang. REWRITE the stale in-tree comments in the SAME commit: nv.c:1902-1913 + nv.c:1925-1956 (A6) and nv.c:2316-2321 (A7) all assert 'rm_cleanup takes a BLOCKING acquire' / 'worker holds the RM API write-lock across the GSP poll' — both FALSE under relaxed locking + post-C6 COND_ACQUIRE; replace with the verified model (worker releases the API lock at kernel_gsp.c:4785 and holds only the GPU group lock across the poll; the lock-free marker is the containment). Also correct the forensics doc lines 33-37 (the 'worker holds the RM API lock across the poll' RCA and the 'deployed self-heals' claim).

DEEP-RM FOOTPRINT = ZERO beyond the existing C1 one-liner. The completion-state discriminator lives entirely in the sovereign kernel-open layer (nv.c A6 timeout branch). This is strictly lower blast radius than A10-v2 (which needed an nv_state_t flag in BOTH nv.h mirrors + an os-abstraction helper-pair + 1 set + 8-10 macro-return clear sites in kernel_gsp_gh100.c — the design under-counted the clear sites, and a missed clear silently arms C2 on a recoverable pre-poll bootstrap failure = a new regression of the same class). The completion-state approach is also lock-model-independent: it does not depend on whether the worker is 'in the poll', only on whether it has returned, so it is immune to the relaxed-locking timing that falsified the entry-arm.

RECOMMENDED gate gating on is_external_gpu for the SINK-suppression (mirroring A11) so non-eGPU dGPU behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged — though the A6 wrapper already only runs for nv->is_external_gpu (nv.c gate at the top of nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded), so the discriminator inherits that gate for free.
fileRefs: ORDERED TOUCH-LIST (delta on fork branch a10-f40b-lockfree-sink HEAD d2a4e514; stack a NEW v2 commit, do NOT amend — v1(contain)→v2(de-regress+completion-gate) is the clean catalog/upstream narrative): (1) kernel-open/nvidia/nv.c — in nv_open_device_for_nvlfp_bounded else/timeout branch: add the bounded grace re-wait wait_for_completion_timeout(&w->done, msecs_to_jiffies(GRACE_MS)) GRACE_MS=50-150 (start 100); wrap BOTH os_pci_set_disconnected(nv->handle) [currently nv.c:1914] AND rm_cleanup_gpu_lost_state(sp,nv,...) [currently nv.c:1919] in 'if (grace re-wait still did NOT complete) { marker; rm_cleanup; }'; keep flush_work nv.c:1957 + rc=-EIO unconditional/outside the gate; rewrite the stale A10/R0 comments nv.c:1902-1913, 1925-1956. (2) src/nvidia/arch/nvalloc/unix/src/osapi.c:1906 — C1 COND_ACQUIRE: already present on a10, KEEP; update its comment to the relaxed-locking model. (3) NO kernel_gsp_gh100.c edit, NO nv.h flag, NO os-interface helper — these A10-v2 deep-RM edits are DROPPED.

INTEGRATION (do not execute live; mechanical): manifest — add row 'A10-f40b-lockfree-sink addon - fork:a10-f40b-lockfree-sink' AFTER the A11 row in /root/nvidia-driver-injector/patches/manifest (currently ends at A11-f45-deadlock-breaker; there is NO A10 row/patch file — A10 was never regen'd; A10-v1 apnex.26 was aborted). Verify a10 still descends from tag 595.71.05 and that regen's nv.c A6-timeout hunk composes cleanly with the existing A6 + A11 hunks (A11 branch carries a6/a7 in nv.c — confirm no double-apply of the timeout branch).

intent doc — author /root/nvidia-driver-injector/docs/patch-intents/A10-f40b-lockfree-sink.md from _template.md (none exists) documenting: C2-contain (v1 lock-free marker), the C1 role, the completion-state de-regression (v2), the relaxed-locking lock model (worker holds GPU group lock not API lock across the poll), the WPR2-fast-fail vs lockdown substrate split, and the C5-internal-sink regression that forced gating the rm_cleanup CALL not just the C2 line.

version cascade — version.mk on a10 currently targets the aborted apnex.26; rebase the v2 bump to apnex.27→apnex.28 (apnex.27 = deployed C6+A11; A10 lands as apnex.28). NVIDIA_NVID_VERSION stays 595.71.05 so the /lib/firmware/nvidia/595.71.05 symlink is unchanged (task #294 does NOT bite this bump) — but still verify the build's firmware symlink resolves before reboot per the 2026-05-22 lesson (Dockerfile firmware-symlink step). Container rebuild: NVIDIA_OPEN_TAG / NVIDIA_NVID_VERSION unchanged.

VALIDATION (safe): BLOCKING deterministic gate = WPR2-fast-fail self-heal n≥3 — passive reads only (lspci/sysfs/dmesg/get-pci-stats, NO nvidia-smi/MMIO post-suspected-sink per the no-RPC-on-broken-BAR1 lesson): assert pdev error_state stays pci_channel_io_normal, subsequent open rc=0 (in-driver self-heal WITHOUT fix-bar1 rebind), PDB_PROP_GPU_IS_LOST not set (drgn). Best-effort-until-fake-5090 (task #290) = lockdown substrate ≥1 fire: worker self-terminates in one poll iteration, flush_work joins <~50ms, -EIO, host alive, ldata_lock released promptly under a concurrent rmmod. HARD BUILD-GATE: confirm C6 is composed above C1 in the apply order (git ancestry does NOT carry C6 into a10; without C6, C1's COND_ACQUIRE hits the inverted primitive and corrupts the global RM API rwsem — task #296). Compile against the real kernel tree (make modules), not just git apply --check (project policy).
openQuestions: GRACE_MS sizing needs a live datapoint: the forensic overshoot is ~5-8ms (worker returns ~205-210ms vs 200ms budget) so a 50ms grace amply covers the fast-fail twin completing, but the lockdown poll-ENTRY time relative to 200ms is INFERRED, never instrumented. A single passive fsync'd marker at gh100.c:1050 entry (or at kgspBootstrap entry) in one contained repro would confirm the lockdown worker is still in-flight after the grace window. If a future datapoint shows the lockdown worker can transiently 'complete' a retry attempt within the grace (kgspBootstrap runs inside the _kgspBootGspRm retry loop, kernel_gsp.c ~5077), the completion check could mis-classify a mid-retry lockdown worker as fast-fail — mitigate by also checking the worker's recorded rc (w->rc): fast-fail returns a boot-fail status and EXITS the open; a lockdown worker stuck in the poll has not populated w->rc at all. Recommend gating on 'completion fired AND w->rc is a recognized init-fail code' for fast-fail, else treat as lockdown.

Whether to ALSO record w->rc / a 'worker_returned' boolean in nv_f40b_open_work for an unambiguous fast-fail-vs-lockdown signal (belt-and-suspenders over the bare completion check), vs relying on the grace re-wait completion alone. The bare completion re-wait is simpler and lock-free; the w->rc check is more robust against the retry-loop edge above. Human call on which to ship first.

The lockdown substrate cannot be triggered on-demand on hardware today (stochastic — fires only when cycle-1 clears WPR2; live re-runs of rung5 are BLOCKED on this very fix). So the lockdown-arm half of the fix cannot be deterministically validated before deploy; it ships validated only by source derivation + the deterministic fast-fail self-heal gate. The fake-5090 F44 model (task #290, deferred) is the realistic on-demand gate. Human decision: deploy apnex.28 on the fast-fail gate + source-derived lockdown reasoning, or hold for fake-5090.

A7 shutdown-path lock model was NOT independently audited here (the shutdown worker runs rm_shutdown_adapter, a GSP unload RPC, ~600ms — a different lock regime than kgspBootstrap). A7 staying unconditional is correct (no fast-fail twin), but its in-tree comment inherits the same falsified 'worker holds the API lock across a GSP poll' premise and should be re-derived rather than inherited; out of scope for the F44 fix but flagged.
